title: Unidentified Miskara (Jorj Car'das)
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Youmay|individual|the [[Miskara|title]]}} {{Update|[[The Unknown Regions]]}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Character |type=Bureaucrat |image= |name=Miskara |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]],{{OutboundFlightDate}} [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]][[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] |species=[[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height=Shorter than [[Jorj Car'das]] |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Vagaari Empire]] **[[Vagaari slaver fleet|Vagaari fleet]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I am the Miskara of the Vagaari people.|The Miskara|}} A [[Gender/Legends|male]] '''Miskara''', and also known as '''The Miskara''' addressed to as "'''Your Eminence'''", was a member and [[Emperor/Legends|ruler]] of the [[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]. In [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]], he led the [[Vagaari slaver fleet|Vagaari fleet]] during the [[Battle of Geroon|battle]] on the [[Geroon (species)|Geroon]] species' [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], as well as their attempt on [[Battle for Outbound Flight|seizing]] the massive [[Starship/Legends|starship]] known as ''[[Outbound Flight]]''. The Vagaari ruler was informed by [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Jorj Car'das]] of [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Force Commander]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]]'s [[Crustai asteroid base|base]], who the Miskara wished to [[Death/Legends|kill]], as the Chiss had [[Skirmish in the Crustai system|defeated]] the Vagaari in the [[Crustai system/Legends|Crustai system]] and at [[Geroon]]. However, although Car'das believed the Miskara had not thought of the possibility of it being a Chiss trap, the Miskara [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] Car'das in a [[Zero-g plastic bubble]] on [[The Miskara's flagship|his flagship]], as he believed the information on the base to be the location of a trap. After arriving at the [[Galactic coordinates/Legends|coordinates]] the Human had provided, the Miskara freed Car'das and used him as his translator during their brief attempt at negotiating with ''Outbound Flight''. However, after the Vagaari began to move towards the starship, [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] [[Commander/Legends|commandeered]] by Thrawn in secret, which had been given to the Miskara as a gift by Car'das, opened fire on the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] of the Vagaari's warship, killing all Vagaari aboard, as well as the Miskara. ==Biography== ===Taking what they wished=== {{Quote|Hey, look, ''[…]'' They have the same bubbles all over their hulls that we saw on the treasure ship.''"
"''Understand the reality of the situation. The Vagaari have killed them, all of them, if not in this battle then in battles to come. There's nothing we can do to help them. All we can do is focus our resources toward the Vagaari's ultimate destruction, so that others may live.|Jorj Car'das and Force Commander Mitth'raw'nuruodo on the Vagaari and their living shields|}} [[File:Outboundthrawn.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Thrawn retrieved a gravity well projector from the Vagaari]] In [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]], the [[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] carried out their nomadic conquests of several [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] to temporarily [[Colonies/Legends|colonize]] for the Vagaari's civilians and to [[Pirate/Legends|plunder]] [[Mineral/Legends|minerals]], and other important items from the colonized worlds. One such world was the [[Geroon (species)|Geroon species']] [[Geroon|homeworld]], which was [[Battle of Geroon|attacked]] by the Vagaari [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. The [[Miskara]] led the attack, commanding over a hundred [[Vagaari warship|warships]] and another hundred [[Transport/Legends|civilian transports]], which transported civilians for the plundering of the Geroon homeworld. As the Geroons fought the Vagaari, they suffered severe casualties. During the attack, [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Force Commander]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]] ([[Core name/Legends|core name]] "Thrawn") of the [[Chiss Expansionary Fleet/Legends|Expansionary Fleet's]] [[Picket Force Two/Legends|Picket Force Two]], visited the homeworld in hopes of studying the Vagaari and acquiring a Vagaari [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projector]]. Aboard Thrawn's [[Warship/Legends|warship]], two [[Human/Legends|Humans]] [[Corellian/Legends|hailing]] from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] planet of [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], [[Jorj Car'das]] and [[Maris Ferasi]]. After realizing one of the gravity well projectors preventing the Geroons from escaping via [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] was protected by a lesser force, Thrawn sent his [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and [[Assault Team One|two]] Assault [[Assault Team Two|teams]] to retrieve the projector and neutralize its crew. As Thrawn and his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Springhawk/Legends|''Springhawk'']] the Vagaari forces attacked them with more severe firepower, eventually causing an attack that almost killed the commander. However, the Humans aboard saved the commander with the help of the ''Springhawk''{{'s}} crew. Although the bridge was partially destroyed thanks to the Vagaari attack, the Chiss retrieved the projector. ===Uncovering a trap=== {{Quote|You will address me as Your Eminence. I'm told you have the insolence to demand that I bargain with you.''"
"''I make no demands of any sort, Your Eminence, ''[…]'' Rather, I'm in terrible difficulty and came here hoping the great and mighty Vagaari people might be willing to come to my assistance. In return for your aid, I hope to offer something you might find of equal value.|The Miskara and Jorj Car'das|}} [[File:Vagaari-tur.png|thumb|right|250px|The Miskara ruled the Vagaari people]] Afterward, Car'das abandoned Thrawn's [[Crustai asteroid base|base]] at the [[Crustai system/Legends|Crustai system]] of the Unknown Regions, and traveled to the Geroon homeworld, where he met with the Miskara. During their meeting, Car'das informed the Miskara that he, along with his companions originating from the Galactic Republic, had lost their way and been captured by the Chiss. The Eminence asked Car'das how he knew of the Vagaari's location, which prompted the Human to reveal that he had been aboard the Chiss warship that had fought in the attack on the Geroon. As the Chiss warship had stolen one of the Vagaari's ship net, as well as the gravity well projector, the Miskara asked Car'das where Thrawn was. However, Car'das's offering was deemed insufficient to the Miskara. Although the Vagaari ruler had lost interest, he found [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|battle droids]], mechanical servants able to attack anyone their leader wanted to kill. Car'das demonstrated the [[Droideka/Legends|Droidekas']] and the battle droids' capabilities on the Geroon slaves, and informed the Eminence how many droids Thrawn possessed. Upon learning of the firepower the droids would grant him, the Vagaari ordered his fleet to go to the Crustai base—in reality, coordinates in which Thrawn would destroy the Vagaari once—however, although Car'das believed the Miskara to have been fooled, the Vagaari imprisoned Car'das in a [[Zero-g plastic bubble]], along with the Geroon [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] on the [[Hull/Legends|hull]] of [[The Miskara's flagship|his warship]] and other vessels. ===Fall of the Vagaari=== {{Quote|But the people there aren't going to just hand them over to you. And those Dreadnaughts pack a lot of firepower.''"
"''Your concern is touching, ''[…]'' But we are the Vagaari. We take what we want.''"
"''Yes, […] So I've heard.|Jorj Car'das and the Miskara|}} [[File:OutboundFlight-cover.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The ruler of the Vagaari wished to capture ''Outbound Flight'']] After leaving the Geroon homeworld through hyperspace, the Vagaari fleet, consisting of two thousand starships, including civilian transports and warships, was brought to realspace before they reached the coordinates Car'das had supplied to the Vagaari. Instead of arriving at the base, they arrived at the location of a battle, a battle between the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi]]-commandeered ''[[Outbound Flight]]'' colony starship and Thrawn's Picket Force Two. The Miskara asked Car'das what he had brought the Vagaari to, and the smuggler suggested that the Chiss had used the "ship net" against the massive starship at the same time that the Vagaari passed through that area. The Miskara dismissed Car'das's concerns on what had brought them there, and upon informing the Miskara of the amount of firepower ''Outbound Flight''{{'s}} [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|Dreadnaughts]] packed, the Miskara told Car'das that his concerns were touching, but that they were the Vagaari, and they took what they wanted. The Miskara, and Car'das, acting as the translator of the Vagaari with the ''Outbound Flight'', attempted to negotiate with the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] aboard. However, although Car'das spoke the Jedi's language, [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], [[Jorus C'baoth]], the commander of the expedition, ignored their attempt. And shortly after, he utilized a [[Force-meld|Force meld]] on all the Vagaari on the warships, destroying their minds slowly. Although the Vagaari had been neutralized, as the Jedi focused on the alien fleet, the Chiss used [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|Vulture droids]], droid starfighters, on the Vagaari's hulls, firing with enough precision to shoot through the hulls without touching the slaves' bubbles. The Miskara and the warships' crew was ultimately killed, as well as the Jedi, but a portion of the Vagaari fleet escaped, avoiding Thrawn's attempt to destroy them. ===Legacy=== {{Quote|Didn't you know, Ambassador? ''[…]'' Bearsh and his friends aren't Geroons. They're Vagaari. ''[…]'' Their voices. ''[…]'' Or rather, their speech, when they spoke in their own language just before their attack. I only heard it once, but it's something I'll never forget.|Chas Uliar|''[[Survivor's Quest]]''}} ''Outbound Flight'' eventually crashed into [[The Redoubt/Legends|the Redoubt]] [[Star cluster/Legends|star cluster]], where a group of members of its crew survived. In [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates ''[[Survivor's Quest]]''{{'s}} events to [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]]. Vagaari [[Supreme Commander (Vagaari)|Supreme Commander]] [[Estosh]], [[General/Legends|General]] [[Bearsh]], and other Vagaari including [[Purpsh]] infiltrated the remains of ''Outbound Flight''. [[Chas Uliar]], one of the ship's survivors, heard the group speak the Vagaari language and discovered that they were not Geroons, whom they were posing as, but Vagaari. Uliar then remarked to [[Dean Jinzler]] that he had only heard the tongue once, when the Miskara spoke it. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Do not beg! ''[…]'' Grubs beg. Inferiors beg. Not beings who would speak and bargain with the Vagaari. If you wish us to help you and your companions, you must find more to offer me.|The Miskara, to Jorj Car'das|}} The Miskara was an arrogant and ruthless member of the Vagaari species. He cared for no one, and was comfortable with killing anyone, which he enjoyed doing, or viewing others doing. He was intelligent, but his greed prevailed when a "prize" was big enough for him. The Miskara knew two words in Basic, "fire" and "target." ==Equipment== The Miskara wore a heavy-looking [[Color/Legends|multicolored]] [[Robe/Legends|robe]] with sunburst shoulder and ankle guards. He also wore a serrated cloak and four belts around his waist. He owned and led his warship. ==Behind the scenes== The Miskara appeared in the [[2006]] novel [[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']], written by [[Timothy Zahn]]. An [[:File:Vagaari-tur.png|image]] from ''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' depicts a member of the Vagaari species with clothing''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' that matches the Miskara's clothing in ''Outbound Flight'', but no connection has been established. ==Appearances== *[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] {{Ab|Outbound Flight (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book and [[Outbound Flight (abridged audiobook)| abridged audiobook]]}} *[[Outbound Flight (abridged audiobook)|''Outbound Flight'' abridged audiobook]] ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Unknown Regions]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Heads of government and state]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Military personnel]] [[Category:Miskaras]] [[Category:Vagaari]] [[Category:Vagaari Empire individuals]]
